stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Researchers in a 2022 study wanted to determine the rate at which 14 spoken languages encoded information and produced phonemes.
  • They calculated the bits of information conveyed per second (the IR).
  • The IR was approximately constant across the 14 languages (an average of 41 bits per second).
  • They calculated the phonemes spoken per second (the PR).
  • The PR varied widely (from 6.1 phonemes per second in Thai to 13.8 in Basque).
stem: The student wants to present an overview of the study's findings. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The 2022 study determined the information rate (IR) of 14 spoken languages in bits of information conveyed per second.
Researchers found that information was conveyed more quickly in Basque, at 13.8 phonemes per second, than in Thai, at 6.1 phonemes per second.
Thai had the slowest phoneme rate, while Basque had the fastest; nevertheless, Basque had the lower information rate of the two.
Although the 14 languages differed sharply in phonemes spoken per second, all of them conveyed information at roughly the same rate.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer because it captures the overall finding: phoneme rates varied widely, while information rate was roughly constant.

Choice A describes only the goal. Choice B misrepresents phoneme rate as information rate. Choice C makes an unsupported comparison about information rate by language.
